How to Deal With Relatives You Hate
If you have relatives you can't stand, you're not alone. Whether your spouse's family drives you nuts or your own siblings are more like arch-rivals than close kin, learn how to deal with relatives you hate. You may never love them, but you can at least learn to tolerate them.
Understand your own feelings.
Your feelings are perfectly normal. You're allowed to feel any emotion, even if it's unpleasant. You don't have to "like" your relatives, but you do have to accept your feelings as normal.
Acknowledge your own faults.
When you don't like someone, it's easy to blame them for everything. In reality, every relationship has two people in it, and you may be doing things to contribute to the problem. Before you point fingers, take a long, hard look in the mirror and ask what you can do to improve the relationship. You may find that you can't change the other person, but you can always work on yourself.
Change your expectations.
We all have a fantasy of what family life should be like. However, few of us have the perfect family. If you can accept your family as they are, you'll be able to be a lot happier.
Don't try to change them.
Your relatives are who they are, and you can't change that. Instead of wasting your time trying to get them to change, accept them for who they are and focus on enjoying your own life.
Set boundaries.
Family relationships are just like any other relationship—you have the right to set boundaries for yourself. If your relatives are asking too much of you or if their habits are making you miserable, speak up and tell them. Just because they're family doesn't mean you have to put up with everything.
Respect your spouse's relationship with their family.
If you're married, your spouse's relationship with their family is just as important as your own. Even if you don't like your in-laws, you should respect the fact that they're important to your spouse. Make sure that you and your spouse spend time with both sets of parents, and try to be civil to them even if you don't like them.
Don't let them ruin holidays.
Holidays are meant to be enjoyed, not ruined by your loathsome relatives. If you find that you can't stand spending time with them, try to spend the holidays with people you actually like. See if you can take a trip with friends instead, or plan a vacation for just you and your spouse.
 Handle arguments with grace.
You're going to disagree with your relatives from time to time. When you do, try to handle the situation with grace and poise. Don't get angry or try to "win" the argument. Instead, calmly state your opinion and move on.
Don't let them get to you.
If your relatives say something that upsets you, do your best to let it roll off your back. Everyone has a right to their own opinions, and you don't have to let yours be changed by others.
Enjoy the good times.
When you do have good times with your relatives, be sure to enjoy them! Even if you don't like them, you may still love some of the holidays and events you share with them. Make the most of these times, and try to look back on them fondly when they're over.
Handle the future with optimism.
No matter how much you hate your relatives, they're not going anywhere. You're going to have to learn how to deal with them for the rest of your life. Instead of dreading the future, try to look forward to it with optimism and hope.
Don't let them hold you back.
Your relatives are not allowed to hold you back from living the life you want. Don't let them control your actions or influence your decisions. You have the right to live your own life, and you should do so with confidence and courage.

We consider a solution to the Klein-Gordon-Hartree equation in energy space for a single massive point-particle in a potential field. In particular we assume that the solution has a singularity at the origin and that the total energy is bounded below. We show that the solution must have the form of aCosW(z)e¯ZW(z) ≡ (e¯W/z) CosW(z), where W(z) is a transcendental function and e¯W ≡ e¯z/z is the inverse function of W(z). The result is exact for any choice of W(z) and has a simple physical interpretation in terms of an oscillating motion of the point-particle in the potential field. We show that the solution is well-defined for a large class of W(z), but that it may fail to exist for some choices of W(z). We also show that the solution is unique in a certain class of W(z), but that it may be non-unique for some choices of W(z). We finally consider the solution to the Klein-Gordon-Hartree equation below the singularity at the origin. We show that this solution is in general non-unique, but that it can be made unique by requiring that the solution is analytic in a neighbourhood of the origin.

The widespread use of personal automobiles has had a significant impact on our society and the environment. While cars provide us with convenience and personal mobility, their detrimental effects cannot be overlooked.
One of the most concerning implications of excessive car usage is air pollution. Cars emit a variety of pollutants, including carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxides, and particulate matter, which contribute to smog, climate change, and respiratory issues in both humans and wildlife. By reducing car usage and promoting alternative transportation methods, we can significantly lessen our environmental impact and help mitigate these harmful effects.
However, the issues caused by automobiles extend beyond air pollution. The construction and maintenance of roads and highways involve land degradation, habitat loss, and potentially dangerous risks to wildlife. Moreover, the use of personal automobiles contributes to congestion, which wastes time, fuel, and causes unnecessary emissions.
Fortunately, there are numerous benefits to be gained from reducing car usage and advocating for alternative transportation methods. Encouraging the use of public transportation, such as buses and trains, can lead to more efficient travel, reduced congestion, and a decrease in overall emissions. Alternatively, promoting active transportation methods like walking and cycling brings about numerous health benefits for individuals, reduces the strain on healthcare systems, and contributes to a cleaner environment.
Alternative transportation methods also bring social benefits. Public transportation systems provide accessible mobility options for individuals who may not own a car, including the elderly, low-income communities, and those with disabilities. This promotes social inclusivity and can help alleviate issues of inequality related to transportation access.
In conclusion, the impact of personal automobiles on society and the environment is profound, affecting everything from air quality and climate change to social inequality. By reducing car usage and embracing alternative transportation methods, we can mitigate these negative effects while reaping a variety of benefits, from reduced congestion and emissions to improved health and social inclusivity. Therefore, it is imperative that we address this issue and work towards finding sustainable transportation solutions for the betterment of our society and the health of our planet.

The advent of driverless cars holds immense promise for a safer, more efficient future. Driverless cars utilize cutting-edge technology, including sensors, artificial intelligence, and GPS, to navigate roadways autonomously, potentially eliminating the risk of human error, which is attributed to over 90% of road accidents. This development could revolutionize road safety, leading to a substantial decrease in vehicular fatalities and injuries, alleviating the burden on healthcare systems and saving countless lives.
Beyond safety, driverless cars can have a significant impact on society. The technology underpinning these vehicles opens up possibilities for innovation in various fields. From improved traffic management systems that could lessen congestion and enhance transportation efficiency to the integration of self-driving vehicles into public transportation networks, revolutionizing the commuting experience.
Furthermore, the development of driverless cars could catalyze job creation in the automotive industry and adjacent sectors. The shift toward autonomous vehicles may necessitate a skilled workforce for the manufacture, maintenance, and operation of these cars, resulting in new job opportunities. Additionally, the scientific advancements driven by this technology could spill over into other domains, fostering further innovation and economic growth.
Notably, the introduction of driverless cars raises social and economic considerations. The shift toward automation could disrupt the employment of professional drivers, including truck drivers and taxi drivers, necessitating the development of strategies to mitigate potential job displacement and facilitate workforce transition. Moreover, the introduction of driverless cars comes with a significant initial cost, which could pose economic challenges, particularly for individuals and businesses with limited resources.
In conclusion, the prospects offered by driverless cars are substantial, ranging from enhanced road safety and technological innovations to potential job creation. However, addressing concerns related to job displacement and economic accessibility is crucial in ensuring a smooth transition to an autonomous driving future. Overall, driverless cars hold the potential to transform society, but doing so will require careful planning and consideration to maximize benefits and mitigate potential negative effects.

How to Form a Rock Band
Rock bands are a great way to help you and your friends enjoy music and perform it live.
Determine the style of music.
The first thing you'll want to do is decide what style of music you want to play. This decision will help you choose instruments and the number of people you need in the band. Whether you want to play jazz, blues, rock, or pop, it's important to agree on the style with your group.
Find band members.
You'll need to find people to join your band. Depending on the style of music you want to play, you'll need to find musicians to play the drums, bass, guitar, and piano. You may also want to find a singer.
If you don't know anyone who plays an instrument, you can post ads in your local paper or on Craigslist. You may also be able to find people through local music stores or by asking friends.
Rehearse.
Once you have the band members you need, it's time to start rehearsing. This will help you get comfortable playing with each other and will help you sound good. Rehearsals are also a good time to work on your songs and figure out how you want to play them.
 Perform live.
After you've rehearsed and are comfortable playing with each other, you can start performing live. This will help you get your name out there and will also help you make money.
Playing live is a great way to help you and your band grow together. It will also help you build a following of fans.

We investigate the excitation spectrum gap and spin-wave stiffness of chains of Ising spins in an XXZ Heisenberg antiferromagnetic material. We use the renormalization-group approach developed by Mostafa anduri for the calculation of the excitation spectrum of Ising spins on a square lattice. Our main results can be summarized as follows. (i) For an antiferromagnetic material with a narrow gap, the stiffness of a spin-wave mode is very large and the corresponding attenuation length is very small. This result is consistent with the strong increase of the stiffness observed in recent experiments on chains of antiferromagnetic spins. (ii) For an antiferromagnetic material with a large gap, the stiffness of a spin-wave mode is small and the corresponding attenuation length is large. This result is consistent with the small increase of the stiffness observed in recent experiments on chains of antiferromagnetic spins. (iii) We show that the stiffness of a spin-wave mode can have a maximum as a function of the frequency and that this maximum is close to the onset of a gap in the excitation spectrum. This result may be relevant for the interpretation of recent experiments on chains of antiferromagnetic spins.

How to Know if You Are Ready for a Dog
So you want a dog. But are you ready for a dog? Having a dog is a lot like having a child. They are cute and sweet, but they need a lot of care and attention. Before you get a dog, you should ask yourself if you are ready for the responsibility.
Are you home most of the day?
Dogs are social animals. They need attention, especially when they are young. If you work long hours, then you may want to wait until you have more time to devote to a dog.
Do you have a fenced-in yard?
Having a fenced-in yard is great. It gives your dog a place to run around and play. If you don't have a fenced-in yard, then you may want to take your dog for walks several times a day.
Do you have any other pets?
Some dogs are great with other animals, while others are not. If you have other pets, like cats or hamsters, then you will want to make sure that the dog you get is also good with other animals.
Are you prepared for the cost?
Dogs are not cheap. There are the initial costs, like the adoption fee or the price of the dog, as well as the ongoing costs, like food and veterinary care. Make sure you are prepared to take on the financial responsibility of a dog before you get one.
Do you know what kind of dog you want?
There are many different types of dogs out there. Some people like big dogs, while others prefer smaller dogs. Some people like energetic dogs, while others prefer more sedate dogs. It's important to know what kind of dog you want before you get one.
Do you know how to train a dog?
Training a dog is important. A well-trained dog is a happy dog. If you don't know how to train a dog, then you may want to wait until you do know how.
Do you have the time?
Dogs are a lot of work. They need attention, exercise, and care. If you are too busy to give a dog the time and attention it needs, then you may want to wait until you have more time.

The Mystery of the Painted Dragon "The Mystery of the Painted Dragon" is a short story by G. K. Chesterton. First published in The Pall Mall Magazine in September 1905, it was collected in The Club of Queer Trades (1905), The Return of the Club of Queer Trades (1911), and The Flying Inn (1914). The story features the character of Professor James Stent, a specialist in cryptozoology, the study of unknown animals. Stent appears in several other Chesterton stories, including "The Sign of the Broken Sword", "The Paradise of Glass", and "The Mask of the Red Death". In this story, Stent is visited by a man who claims to have seen a dragon, and describes it in detail. Stent, who does not believe in the existence of dragons, is convinced by the man's description, and decides to investigate. Stent discovers that the man is a fraud, and was describing a costume he had seen at a local fair. However, Stent also discovers that the costume was designed by a friend of his, who was unaware of the man's fraud. Stent decides not to reveal the truth to his friend, and instead enjoys the reputation of being a man who has studied the mysterious and possibly mythical painted dragon.

How to Build a Brick Wall
A brick wall is a great way to separate space, while also adding a classic touch. This guide will give you some tips on how to build a brick wall.
Determine the desired height of your wall. Keep in mind that you will need to stack bricks on top of each other to reach the desired height.
igures the number of bricks you will need to build your wall. You will need to multiply the height of your wall by the width of your wall to get the total number of bricks.
Collect the necessary materials. In addition to bricks, you will need mortar, a trowel, a bricklayer's trowel, a level, and a hose with a nozzle.
Plan the location of your wall. Make sure that the wall will be level and plumb.
Dig a trench for the foundation of your wall. The trench should be as wide as the wall is tall.
 Pour concrete into the trench and let it cure for at least a day.
Spread a layer of mortar on the top of the cured concrete.
Place the first course of bricks on top of the mortar, making sure that they are level and plumb.
Use the trowel to apply mortar to the sides of the bricks.
Place the next course of bricks on top of the first course, staggering the joints between the courses.
Use the bricklayer's trowel to apply mortar to the top of the wall.
Place the final course of bricks on top of the wall, making sure that they are level and plumb.
Use the hose with a nozzle to wet the bricks and mortar. This will help the wall to cure properly.
Allow the wall to cure for at least a day before using it.

There are benefits to both teacher-designed and student-designed summer projects. Ultimately, however, I believe that student-designed projects are the better of the two choices because they promote deeper engagement and more intrinsic motivation. 
When a teacher designs a summer project, they have the benefit of expertise and experience in creating learning opportunities. The curriculum can be tailored to address specific gaps in student knowledge or to deepen understanding of a particular topic. Additionally, teacher-designed projects may offer more structure and guidance, which can be particularly helpful for students who struggle with motivation or self-direction. 
On the other hand, student-designed summer projects empower students to take ownership of their learning. Encouraging students to choose projects that align with their personal interests and goals fosters engagement and motivation. When students have a genuine interest in a topic, they are more likely to invest time and effort into their projects and explore ideas deeply. Additionally, this approach can encourage students to pursue topics that may not necessarily be covered in traditional classrooms, allowing for a broader range of interests and passions. 
Student-designed projects can also provide opportunities for students to develop important skills such as problem-solving, creativity, and self-advocacy. When students are in charge of their own learning, they may need to seek out resources, navigate challenges, and find creative solutions, leading to a deeper and more personalized learning experience. 
While there might be a concern that students lack the knowledge or skill to design effective projects, this can be mitigated by providing support and guidance throughout the process. Teachers can collaborate with students, helping them to develop goals, identify resources, and reflect on their progress. 
In conclusion, although teacher-designed projects have their advantages, I believe that student-designed summer projects are more effective in promoting learning and motivation. By allowing students to pursue their interests, personalize their learning experiences, and develop important skills, student-designed projects can empower them to take charge of their education and engage with their summer learning in a meaningful way.

The Electoral College is a fundamental feature of the United States election process and system, and there are several compelling reasons to retain it. Here are three key arguments in favor of keeping the Electoral College:
1. Representation and Federalism: The Electoral College reflects the foundational principle of federalism in the US political system. It ensures that each state has a voice in the election of the President, recognizing the diversity of interests and populations across the country. Unlike a direct popular vote, the Electoral College provides representation for smaller states and safeguards against the potential dominance of more populous states in deciding presidential elections.
2. Campaign Strategies and Democracy: The Electoral College encourages candidates to cultivate broad-based appeal across the nation rather than focusing solely on populous states or demographic strongholds. This encourages more thoughtful campaign strategies and outreach to diverse voter groups. Without the pressure to win landslides in a few states, candidates may need to develop more inclusive platforms and interact with a wider range of American communities, potentially leading to more representative leadership.
3. Consensus and Majority Rule: The Electoral College operates as a safeguard against narrow, geographically concentrated interests dominating the presidential election. To win, a candidate must secure a majority of electoral votes, which requires gaining support across multiple states. This mechanism encourages consensus-building and perhaps fosters more moderation in candidates' platforms, leading to more transparent and trustworthy victories. 
Overall, the Electoral College plays a crucial role in maintaining the balance between state and national interests, encouraging diverse representation and thoughtful campaign strategies. While there may be ongoing debates about potential reforms, the current system upholds the values of federalism, majority rule, and inclusive democracy that are integral to the United States.

Greek immigration to Detroit would increase during the 1920s, and the city's Greek population would reach 16,000 by 1930. In the 1950s and 1960s, many Greeks in Detroit became successful business owners and professionals. Several Greeks in Detroit also rose to prominence in the city's political scene, and contributed to the city's politics and government, including former Detroit Mayor Dennis Archer and former Michigan Lieutenant Governor John Cherry. In the 1960s and 1970s, a large Greek community also developed in the nearby suburb of Oak Park. A narcissist is someone who is extremely vain and self-centered. They tend to be very confident and have a high opinion of themselves. This can be a good thing in some situations, but it can also cause problems when they are dealing with other people. This article will teach you how to deal with a narcissist so that you can avoid any potential problems.
When dealing with a narcissist, it's important to remember that they are people too. They may have a high opinion of themselves, but that doesn't mean they're not entitled to their feelings. So, when dealing with a narcissist, it's important to be respectful of their feelings and to try to understand where they're coming from.
It's also important to set boundaries with a narcissist. This will help you avoid any potential problems or conflicts. For example, if you're dealing with a narcissist who is always late, you might want to set a boundary of being punctual. This will help you avoid any potential problems or conflicts.
It's also important to be patient with a narcissist. This is because they may take longer to process information or to make decisions. So, when dealing with a narcissist, it's important to be patient and to give them the time they need to make a decision.

How to Make a Birthday Cake Pop up Card (Robert Sabuda Method)
This pop-up card is based on one designed by Robert Sabuda.
What You'll Need
One 8 1/2 x 11-inch piece of heavy weight paper (or thin cardboard)
Two sheets of construction paper (or thin cardboard)
Four pieces of thin cardboard (such as a cereal box)
One piece of dp paper (or scrapbook paper)
Scanner (or photocopier)
 Pencil
Ruler
X-Acto knife (with adult help!)
How to Make a Birthday Cake Pop up Card (Robert Sabuda Method)
Scan or photocopy the cake template (enlarged to the desired size) and trace it onto the back of one of the construction paper sheets. Cut out the cake.
Fold the cake in half, and then fold it in half again the other way to score the cake. Unfold the cake, and then fold the corners to the center, creasing well.
On the second construction paper sheet, draw a line down the middle of the sheet (lengthwise). Cut along the line.
Fold each of the four pieces of thin cardboard in half (widthwise).
Draw a line down the middle of one of the cardboard pieces (lengthwise). Cut along the line.
Lay the cardboard piece with the window on top of the other three cardboard pieces.
Glue the back of the cake to one of the cardboard pieces, making sure the "windows" line up.
Glue the other cardboard piece with the window to the back of the cake.
Glue the remaining two cardboard pieces to the back of the cake.
Glue the second construction paper sheet (the one you cut in half) to the back of the cake.
Punch two holes on opposite sides of the card, and tie a ribbon through the holes.
Write a message on the inside of the cake.

Challa Vamshi Chand Reddy (born 4 April 1965) is a retired Indian professional wrestler and actor who primarily worked in Telugu-language films. He has also worked in a few Tamil, Hindi, and Kannada language films. He was the first Indian to win the World Cup Wrestling Championship in London in 1988. He is a recipient of the prestigious Arjuna Award, the second highest civilian award in India, for his achievements in the field of wrestling. After retiring from wrestling, Reddy began working as a fitness trainer and also worked in the Indian film industry as an actor and producer. He has acted in over 125 feature films in several languages. ## Early life and education Challa Vamshi Chand Reddy was born on 4 April 1965 in bubblempam, Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh, India, to a Telugu-speaking family. His father, Chandrasekhar Reddy, was a forest ranger and his mother, Lakshmi, was a housewife. He has two sisters and a brother. Reddy was enrolled in the Anglo Indian School in Chennai for his schooling, and then attended the Meenakshi College in the city to pursue a bachelor's degree in commerce. He was a member of the college wrestling team, and also won several accolades in local and national-level competitions. He was subsequently awarded a sports scholarship at the University of Delhi, where he pursued a master's degree in commerce. ## Career ### Wrestling Reddy made his wrestling debut at the junior level in 1985, at the South Indian Wrestling Championship held in Bangalore. He won the title in the 130 kg category. He then won the gold medal at the National Wrestling Championship in Calcutta in 1986, and a silver medal at the Commonwealth Wrestling Championship in Edinburgh the same year. In 1986, he was also awarded the Arjuna Award by the President of India, Giani Zail Singh, making him the first sportsperson from Andhra Pradesh to receive the honour. In 1987, he won the gold medal at the World Wrestling Championships held in Los Angeles. The same year, he won the gold medal at the Asian Wrestling Championships in Bangkok, Thailand. In 1988, he won the gold medal at the World Cup Wrestling Championship in London. He also won the silver medal at the Asian Wrestling Championships in New Delhi that year. Reddy retired from competitive wrestling in 1990, and began working as a coach. In 1992, he was appointed the coach of the Indian wrestling team at the Barcelona Summer Olympics. ### Film industry Reddy made his film debut in 1991, with a supporting role in the Tamil film, Nanbargal, which was followed by a lead role in the Telugu film, Peddarikam. He then appeared in a series of low-budget Telugu films, including the drama, Evandi Avide Evananta (1992) and the action film, Kadana (1994). He made a comeback in the 2002 drama film, Mitr (2002), after a five-year break, and then appeared in the multilingual film, Three Monkeys (2002). In 2003, he starred in the telugu movie, Ottesi Cheputunna, which won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Telugu. In 2008, he appeared in the live-action adaptation of the Disney animated film, Wall-e, where he played the role of Captain Bhawohick. ## Personal life Reddy married Jayalakshmi in 1989, with whom he has two sons. ## Awards ### Sports * Arjuna Award * National Wrestling Championship gold medal – 1986 * World Wrestling Championship gold medal – 1987 * Commonwealth Wrestling Championship silver medal – 1986 * Asian Wrestling Championship gold medal – 1987 * World Cup Wrestling Championship gold medal – 1988 * Asian Wrestling Championship silver medal – 1988 ### Film * National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Telugu – Ottesi Cheputunna (2003) * Nandi Award for Best Supporting Actor – Nuvve Nuvve (2001) ## Filmography | Year | Notes | Film | Language | Role | | --

How to Manage Belly Button Rings During Pregnancy
During pregnancy, you must take special care of your belly button rings. If you don't, there is a chance of damaging your fetus or causing a miscarriage. 
If you're unsure what to do, keep reading for more information on how to manage your belly button rings during pregnancy.
We will also provide information on whether or not it is safe to have a belly button ring while pregnant.
The blog will also provide information on what to do if you want to remove your belly button ring during pregnancy.
Is it Safe to Have a Belly Button Ring While Pregnant?
When you're pregnant, your body undergoes a lot of changes. Some of these changes can be uncomfortable, like heartburn and indigestion. You may also experience some pain and discomfort in your belly button area.
If you have a belly button ring, it's important to take extra care of it during pregnancy. First, it's important to note that there is no scientific evidence that belly button rings cause a miscarriage. 
However, there is some evidence that they can cause an infection. If you have a belly button ring and are pregnant, it's important to keep the area clean and dry. You should also avoid touching the ring too much, as this can increase your risk of infection.
Additionally, it's important to note that as your belly grows, so will the size of your belly button ring. This means that it may become more difficult to keep the ring clean and dry. If this is the case, it may be best to remove the ring for the duration of your pregnancy.
How to Remove a Belly Button Ring During Pregnancy
If you want to remove your belly button ring during pregnancy, it's important to do so safely. First, you should consult with your doctor to ensure that it is safe for you to do so.
Once you have received medical clearance, there are a few things you should keep in mind. First, it's important to have someone else remove the ring for you.
This is because it can be difficult to reach the ring while you are pregnant. Second, you should avoid touching the ring as much as possible.
This will reduce your risk of infection. Finally, you should clean the area with soap and water after the ring has been removed.

We propose a model for Bondi accretion in the early universe in which the accretion rate varies harmonically in space. In our model the amplitude of the spatial variation is small compared to the typical size of the universe, so the variance of the accretion rate is simply proportional to the average accretion rate. We show that this model can explain the observed correlation between the large-scale structure of the universe and the small-scale structure of the primordial perturbations. We also show that this model can naturally explain why the universe is so homogeneous on large scales. We find that this model can quantitatively reproduce the observed power spectrum, without the need to introduce non-Gaussian corrections. Our model also naturally explains why the correlation function is positive on large scales, without the need for the often invoked anti-correlation mechanism. In addition, our model can also explain why the current universe is so homogeneous on large scales. We show that our model predicts that the galaxies should be distributed in a way that is consistent with the observed large-scale structure, without the need to introduce the cold dark matter model.

How to Draw a Girl's Face/Head That You May Like
 At the top of your paper, draw a small circle. This will be the guide for the top of the head.
 Draw a vertical line and a horizontal line that equally segments the circle into quarters.
 Draw a square within the circle, such that each corner touches the circle. This square will be your guide for the girl's face.
 Draw two lines extending from the top right corner and the bottom left corner of the square to the upper right and lower left corners of the circle. These lines will be your guides for the girl's hair.
 Draw the girl's face within the square, starting with the chin at the bottom line of the square. Draw two lines extending from the bottom line of the square to create the sides of the face.
 Draw the girl's eyes, nose, and mouth within the face. The eyes should be above the center line of the square. The mouth should be below the center line of the square. The nose should be drawn between the eyes and the mouth.
 Draw the girl's hair. Within the boundaries of the hair guides, draw different shapes and lines to create the girl's hairstyle.
 Color your drawing. Use different shades of color to create highlights and shadows on the girl's face and hair.

Sheep's trotters Sheep's trotters, also known as sheep's feet or lamb's trotters, are the feet of sheep. As a dish, the term refers to the meat from the feet of sheep, usually boiled or braised in a sauce. In the United States, the dish is popular in the cuisine of the Southern United States, where it is usually prepared unadorned, and in the cuisine of the Mid-Atlantic region, where it is often served in a white sauce with lemon juice. In the United Kingdom, sheep's trotters are popular in the cuisines of Northern England, Ireland, and Scotland, where they are often served in a brown sauce with onions. Sheep's trotters are also popular in France, where they are prepared in a similar fashion to the way the French prepare pig's trotters. In the Jewish cuisine of France, sheep's trotters are sometimes served with the traditional side dish of grated carrots. In the Dominican Republic, sheep's trotters are a common dish, where they are cooked with onions, garlic, and spices, and then fried. In some cultures, sheep's trotters are seen as a delicacy, and are thus often expensive.

Isotopes of nobelium The isotopes of nobelium (98U) range from those with atomic weights of 97 u to 102 u. The primary decay mode of nobelium is alpha decay, and the main products of this process are isotopes of plutonium. The half-life of nobelium is 41.5 ms. No stable isotopes of nobelium are known, although several radioactive isotopes have been discovered. The longest-lived are 98Np and 98MNO (half-life: 2.9 s). The most stable radioactive isotopes are 98Mc and 98Ru (half-life: about 1.5 min). Many other radioactive isotopes have been produced, either as artificial isotopes or as short-lived radioactive isotopes that were generated as intermediate products in the synthesis of other elements. The primary utility of nobelium isotopes is in the production of other elements, most often plutonium. Plutonium-238 is produced from the decay of uranium-238, and the resulting plutonium-239 is sometimes used as fuel in nuclear reactors. Plutonium-240 is a by-product of this process, and it has been used as a component of nuclear weapons. Plutonium-241 is also a by-product, and it is used in radioisotope thermoelectric generators.

Contingency plans for an invasion of Britain, code-named Operation Sea Lion, were already in place. The British knew this, and thus were keen to reach an accommodation with Germany to avoid such a fate. Operation Sea Lion was scheduled for the summer of 1940, but was postponed indefinitely following the German defeat in the Battle of Britain. The threat of invasion was used by British Prime Minister Winston Churchill to rally the British people and dissuade them from attempting to make a separate peace with Germany. Operation Sea Lion was never a serious possibility after the Battle of Britain, as the British would have been able to deploy significant land forces in the defense of Britain. However, the possibility of a German-occupied Britain did lead to Operation Unthinkable, which would have involved the bombing of German cities and a scorched earth policy had the Germans ever reached the outskirts of London.

Romolo Valli (5 February 1927 – 19 January 2020) was an Italian actor, voice actor, director, teacher and drama critic. Born in Parma, Romolo Valli attended the Parmesan section of the Ghilda Dalla Costa Drama School, then active at the Teatro Sociale in Parma, where he obtained his first theatre experiences. He then enrolled at the Actor's Studio in London, and upon his return to Italy he started working with the Compagnia dei Ragazzi, a theatre company for young people which he directed from 1954 to 1956. He also began a long collaboration with the Festival dei Due Mondi in Spoleto, as actor, director and teacher. In 1957, Valli made his television debut, with the RAI drama La donna si diventa, then worked with the Milan-based company Il Sistema Theatro, and in 1961 he had his first role at the Piccolo Teatro in Milan, with the show I puri amanti. In 1962, he moved to Rome to work at the Teatro Nazionale, then in 1965 he started a long collaboration with the great director Giorgio Strehler, at the Piccolo Teatro in Milan, with which he toured Italy and the world, and at the Teatro Stabile in Turin. In the early 1960s, Valli also started appearing in films, with some important roles in movies by Pietro Germi, Luigi Comencini, Pietro Martellucci, Nanni Loy and Franco Indovina. In 1968, he starred in L'età del malessere, by Carlo Lizzani, and in 1970 he had a main role in L'Explosion, by Tinto Brass. In 1972, Valli was one of the founders of the Gruppo Straniero, along with Alessandro Haber, Massimo Gotta, Paolo Guidi, Carlo Giuffrè, Lorenzo Gnerre and Massimo Wertmüller. The group, who later became the Scapolati, was active until the mid-1970s and produced several shows, including Non tutto è vero, arrestedi, everything is not true, arrested, in 1973, and Controra, in 1974. In the following years, Valli focused his activities on theatre and television, appearing in numerous TV-series, and in 1988 he returned to the Piccolo Teatro in Milan, with the show The Golden Coach, an adaptation of the play by Molière, which he also directed. In the 1990s, he also started teaching, and in 1999 he was appointed head of the Scuola Holden in Turin, a school of performing arts. In 2000, Valli took part in the TV-series Incantesimo. In 2002, he was awarded the Golden Lion for his career achievements at the Venice Biennale. In 2010, he was the voice of the main character's father in the animated movie Tangled, and in 2013 he was the voice of the minister in the animated movie Frozen.

Feudal Japan was able to quickly industrialize and Westernize in the 19th century due to a combination of factors, including its geography, political structure, and cultural values.
Geographically, Japan is an island nation with limited natural resources and a small domestic market. This forced the Japanese to focus on exports and imports to meet their needs, which in turn encouraged them to become more industrialized and Westernized. The country's geography also made it difficult for foreign powers to exert control over Japan, which allowed the Japanese to maintain their cultural identity and develop their own unique form of modernization.
Politically, Japan was ruled by a feudal system for much of its history, which gave local lords (daimyo) significant power and autonomy. This system encouraged competition and innovation among the daimyo, who were constantly seeking ways to increase their wealth and power. This competition led to the development of new technologies and industries, such as textiles, ironworking, and ship-building, which in turn helped to industrialize and Westernize Japan.
Culturally, the Japanese valued hard work, discipline, and education, which helped to drive the country's modernization. The Japanese also had a strong sense of national identity and a desire to protect their culture and traditions, which helped to balance the country's modernization with its cultural heritage.
In contrast, China was unable to quickly industrialize and Westernize in the 19th century due to a combination of factors, including its geography, political structure, and cultural values.
Geographically, China is a large country with vast domestic markets and abundant natural resources. However, the country's geography also made it difficult for the Chinese to effectively communicate and trade with each other, which limited the development of a unified economy and society. In addition, China's geography made it vulnerable to foreign invasion and control, which discouraged the Chinese from pursuing their own form of modernization and forced them to focus on defending their country instead.
Politically, China was ruled by a series of dynasties for much of its history, which made it difficult to establish a stable and unified government that could drive the country's modernization. In addition, the Chinese were traditionally a conservative and inward-looking people, who were resistant to change and reluctant to accept foreign ideas and technologies. This made it difficult for the Chinese to quickly adapt to the changing world and to industrialize and Westernize their country.
Culturally, the Chinese valued stability, order, and tradition, which made them reluctant to embrace the chaos and change that often accompanied industrialization and Westernization. In addition, the Chinese had a strong sense of cultural identity and a desire to protect their culture and traditions, which made them resistant to foreign influences and ideas.
Overall, the combination of Japan's geography, political structure, and cultural values allowed the country to quickly industrialize and Westernize in the 19th century. In contrast, China's geography, political structure, and cultural values made it difficult for the country to do the same, and the Chinese were unable to quickly adapt to the changing world and to industrialize and Westernize their country.

"Go Bang!" is the debut single by American rapper 2 Chainz. It was released as the first single from his debut album Based on a T.R.U. Story on August 14, 2012, via Def Jam Recordings. The production on the song was handled by K.E. on the Track. The song features a guest verse from fellow rapper Lil Wayne, who also signed 2 Chainz to his label Young Money Entertainment in 2011. "Go Bang!" is built around a sample of the song "Cardova" by Slick Rick, which itself samples the song "The Great American Dream" by Legal Drug Push. The song's instrumental was featured in a previous track by Young Money artist Jae Millz, titled "Dip Dat". "Go Bang!" was originally intended to be the first single from a deluxe version of Lil Wayne's album I Am Not a Player, but was instead released as 2 Chainz's debut single. The song received generally positive reviews from music critics, who praised the production and the guest verse from Lil Wayne. The song was featured on the soundtrack to the 2012 movie Project X. A music video, directed by Alex Nazari, was released on September 17, 2012. The video features a cameo appearance from American rapper and actor Terrence Howard. "Go Bang!" peaked at number 49 on the Billboard Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart, and number 23 on the Hot Rap Songs chart. 2 Chainz performed the song live on The Late Show with David Letterman on September 18, 2012, alongside Lil Wayne. A remix version of the song, featuring American rapper Rick Ross, was released on October 2, 2012. Another remix version of the song, featuring American rapper Juicy J, was released on October 16, 2012. In January 2013, "Go Bang!" was included in XXL's list of the best songs of 2012. In June 2013, Complex named "Go Bang!" the 14th best song of 2012. In September 2013, Rolling Stone named "Go Bang!" the tenth best song of 2012. In October 2013, Spin named "Go Bang!" the fifth best song of 2012.

The United States purchased Alaska from Russia in 1867. The treaty that was signed in March of 1867 stated that Russia was selling Alaska to the United States for 7.2 million dollars. The United States Senate eventually ratified the treaty on April 9, 1868.

Battle of Reims (1814) The Battle of Reims was a battle during the Hundred Days phase of the Napoleonic Wars. It took place on 13 February 1814 and was a victory for the French under Marshals Marmont and Mortier against the Allies under Prince Schwarzenberg. After Napoleon's abdication, the French garrisons in the occupied Netherlands were withdrawn to France to help defend the country against the advancing Allies. As the Allied forces advanced into northern France, they won a number of victories, and by early February they were approaching the city of Reims. The French troops in the city were under the command of General Jean-Claude Falck, who decided to make a stand there. He was joined by two Marshals, Marmont and Mortier, who took over the command. The Allies were commanded by Prince Schwarzenberg. After some initial successes by the Allies, the French counter-attacked and drove them back. The Allies were forced to retreat to the east, leaving Reims to the French. The battle was a victory for the French, although they had only around 30,000 men at the start of the battle, while the Allies had around 100,000. After the battle, the French pursued the Allies back to the Aisne river. A week later, the two armies met again at the Battle of Craonne.

The Irish Republican Army (IRA) is a political and military organisation that seeks to bring about the unity of Ireland. It is perhaps best known for its role in the Northern Ireland conflict, where it fought against the British state and loyalist paramilitaries.
The IRA has been credited with developing modern guerilla tactics, which were used to great effect in the Northern Ireland conflict. These tactics included the use of roadside bombs, sniper attacks, and ambushes. The IRA's tactics were often innovative and were designed to maximise the effectiveness of their limited resources.
One of the key elements of the IRA's tactics was the use of asymmetric warfare, which involves using unconventional tactics and strategies to defeat a larger, more conventional force. This was a key element of the Vietcong's strategy in the Vietnam War, and the IRA's use of asymmetric warfare was often inspired by the Vietcong's tactics.
The IRA also made use of traditional Irish warfare strategies, such as the use of currachs (small boats) for coastal operations and the use of dugouts for underground operations. These strategies were often used in combination with modern guerilla tactics to create a highly effective fighting force.
In addition to its military strategies, the IRA also made use of a range of political strategies, including the use of propaganda and the organisation of political protests. These strategies were used to gain support for the IRA's cause and to put pressure on the British government to withdraw from Northern Ireland.
Overall, the IRA's use of modern guerilla tactics, combined with its political strategies, was a key factor in its success in the Northern Ireland conflict. Its tactics were highly effective in defeating a much larger and more conventional force, and its political strategies helped to gain support for its cause and put pressure on the British government.

Creating CSS
Creating CSS is a breeze with an editor like Dreamweaver.
Open a new document.
 A new, blank page will open in the editing window.
Define the document's size and resolution.
 Do this by going to the "Page" menu, and selecting "Page Properties." The Page Properties window will open.
Change the page's size.
 You can do this by changing the values in the "Size" section. For example, you might want to change the width to 800 pixels and the height to 600 pixels.
Change the resolution.
 This is usually set to 72 pixels per inch ( PPI ). You can change it to something else, but it's not usually necessary.
Click OK.
 The Page Properties window will close, and the changes you made will be applied to the document.
Add a CSS stylesheet.
 Do this by going to the "Format" menu, and selecting "CSS." The CSS Editor will open.

We consider the properties of the volume operator in loop quantum gravity. We show that the naive definition of the volume operator as the integral of the spatial derivative of the physical field is inconsistent with the definition of the Beltrami-Klippererminde operator in terms of a polar decomposition. We show that this inconsistency can be resolved by introducing a non-trivial constraint operator which is an integral over the spatial derivative of the physical field. The resulting volume operator has a correct Beltrami-Klippererminde decomposition. We show that the non-trivial constraint operator is related to the Gram-Schmidt or Levi-Civita operator, depending on whether the spin connection is introduced as a non-degenerate symmetric two-form or as a general symmetric two-form with a non-trivial symmetric part. This resolves the question of the relative nature of the volume operator in loop quantum gravity, which was previously unresolved. We show that the volume operator in loop quantum gravity is the current volume operator in curved space-time, up to an overall factor which is related to the dimension of the underlying space-time and the signature of the space-time. We show that the constraint operator can be implemented as a symmetry generator, which relates the volume operator in loop quantum gravity to the induced volume operator in effective loop quantum gravity. We show that this symmetry generator can be related to the signature of the space-time. We show that the volume operator in loop quantum gravity is related to the twistor-Weyl volume operator in the sense that their projections onto any space-time-profile-frame are related by a universal constant which does not depend on the choice of the space-time-profile-frame.

Extracurricular activities come with numerous benefits for students, making a strong case for requiring them to participate in such activities. One significant advantage is the opportunity for students to explore their interests and passions outside of the traditional classroom setting. Through extracurricular activities, students can discover new talents, develop existing skills, and cultivate a sense of personal identity and self-expression. This exploration helps them understand their interests and passions, aiding in career and academic pathway choices. 
Another crucial benefit is the development of critical life skills through extracurricular activities. These activities often demand teamwork, communication, time management, and leadership, equipping students with skills that are highly valuable in the real world. By participating in extracurricular activities, students have the chance to apply these skills in a practical setting, fostering their personal and professional growth. 
Furthermore, extracurricular activities contribute to students' overall well-being by providing opportunities for physical exercise and social interaction. Engaging in physical activity promotes healthy habits and helps reduce stress and anxiety, leading to happier and more balanced individuals. Socializing with peers in extracurricular settings fosters interpersonal skills and nurtures friendships, supporting students' emotional and mental well-being. 
Considering these extensive benefits, it is evident that extracurricular activities should be strongly encouraged, if not mandatory, for students. By participating in such activities, students can actively invest in their personal and professional development, thereby enhancing their prospects for success in the real world. 
Nonetheless, it is imperative to ensure that students have access to a diverse range of extracurricular activities, catering to different interests and backgrounds. Equitable opportunities should be granted, allowing students to explore avenues that align with their passions, regardless of their financial or social circumstances. 
In conclusion, extracurricular activities significantly benefit students' personal growth, skill development, and well-being. Making participation in such activities mandatory can substantially empower students to become well-rounded individuals prepared to navigate and succeed in the real world. 
Ensure consideration of diverse interests and equitable opportunities, and the implementation of this requirement can yield positive outcomes for students across various contexts.

Park Hyeon-Joo (Hangul: 박현주; born December 8, 1994), better known by his stage name Swings (Hangul: 스윙스), is a South Korean rapper. He was a member of the hip hop duo Mooncusser with fellow rapper Nucksal, and has since become a solo artist. In October 2014, Swings released his first solo mini-album, Swings Project. The album consisted of songs that were previously released on his personal SoundCloud account, and included the hit singles "Hate It", "Wings" and "Higher". In January 2015, Swings released his second mini-album, Swings Project II, with the lead single "Go Away". In May 2015, Swings' collaborative single with South Korean rapper Loco, "Haitai", was released. The music video featured Canadian singer Daniel Caesar. In July 2015, Swings released his first full-length album, Mood Swings. The title track, "Dumb Dumb", featured vocals by South Korean singer Kim Bum-soo. In October 2015, Swings released his second full-length album, Mood Swings II, with the lead single "No Manners" featuring vocals by South Korean singer Kim Bum-soo. In January 2016, Swings released his third full-length album, Mood Swings III, with the lead single "Hometime" featuring vocals by South Korean singer Kim Bum-soo. In May 2016, Swings released his first EP, titled This Is Not A Playlist. In January 2017, Swings released his second EP, titled Another Note. In January 2018, Swings released his third EP, titled Swings Is Back.

We study the possibility of sending a charged dust ball through the Reissner--Nordstr\"{o}m wormhole. Our main result is that, in contrast to the situation for a point-like charged particle, a charged dust ball can in general not be sent through the wormhole. The main reasons for this are the following: (1) If the electric field in the wormhole is not zero, the dust ball will be attracted to one of the sides of the wormhole and can in general not be sent through the wormhole. (2) If the electric field in the wormhole is zero, we must have that the electric field in the external space is also zero. Therefore, it is in general not possible to send a positively charged dust ball through the wormhole, as the positive charge will attract the dust ball to the wormhole. (3) In the special case where the dust ball is made up of only two point-like charges, the dust ball can in general not be sent through the wormhole, as the two charges will be attracted to each other and will in general not be able to pass each other in the wormhole.

We have proposed and developed a new method to search for non-standard interactions of neutrinos using supernova neutrinos as the probe. Our method is based on the fact that the standard interaction of neutrinos with matter results in a small energy deposition, while non-standard interactions can produce much larger responses. We have constructed a search strategy that is sensitive to such large responses and is able to reject the standard model (SM) background. We have set the best current limit on non-standard interactions of neutrinos with matter and have provided the most sensitive search method for such interactions. We have also proposed a new method to search for non-standard neutrino interactions with other neutrinos which is based on the correlation of the arrival times of neutrinos at different detectors. This method can search for non-standard interactions that produce an energy deposition which is not correlated with the neutrino energy. We have found that the current limit set by this method already excludes a large class of such non-standard interactions. We have also proposed a new method to search for sterile neutrinos which is based on the correlation of the arrival times of neutrinos at different detectors. This method can search for the non-standard interaction of sterile neutrinos with other neutrinos which is not possible with the current methods. We have shown that this method can set a much stricter limit on the non-standard interaction of sterile neutrinos with active neutrinos than the current methods.

When making important decisions, seeking advice from multiple sources can be immensely beneficial. This approach provides us with a broader perspective, helping us to make more informed and wiser decisions. Here are several advantages of seeking advice from multiple sources and the positive impact it can have on the decision-making process:
Diversity of Views: By seeking advice from different people, we expose ourselves to a variety of perspectives, experiences, and opinions. This diversity of views allows us to understand the issue from various angles and consider possibilities that we might have missed otherwise. Different individuals may approach the problem with distinct biases, strengths, and insights, enriching our understanding of the situation.
Collaborative Decision-Making: When we involve multiple sources in the decision-making process, it becomes a collaborative effort. This collaborative approach fosters communication and encourages active participation. By engaging with others, we can exchange ideas, debate pros and cons, and work through conflicting viewpoints. The collective intelligence has the potential to lead to more innovative and effective solutions than decisions made independently.
Critical Evaluation: Seeking advice from multiple sources encourages us to critically evaluate the information and opinions we receive. We become more adept at discerning valuable insights from others and discerning trustworthy sources. By engaging with different perspectives, we can assess the validity of each argument and make more objective decisions, rather than solely relying on intuition or circumstantial evidence.
Increased Confidence: Seeking advice from multiple sources can boost our confidence in making decisions. When we have diverse opinions supporting a particular choice, we become more assured in our actions. This confidence stems from the fact that we have mitigated potential biases or blind spots in our decision-making by incorporating different viewpoints. However, it is crucial to ensure that the confidence stems from a balanced evaluation of information rather than mere confirmation bias.
Ultimately, seeking advice from multiple sources leads to better decision-making by providing us with diverse viewpoints, fostering collaboration, encouraging critical evaluation, and increasing our confidence in the chosen course of action. While it is important to be discerning and listen to trustworthy sources, embracing this approach can enhance our decision-making capabilities in both personal and professional contexts.

Learning about the various computational techniques that are used in chemistry can be a daunting task. There are many different methods that can be used to solve problems in chemistry, and it can be difficult to know where to start. This wikihow will provide an overview of the most common computational techniques that are used in chemistry, and will provide some resources for learning more about each method.
The first step in learning about computational techniques is to understand the basics of chemistry. This includes learning about the various elements and compounds that make up the world around us. Once you have a good understanding of the basics, you can begin to learn about the various computational techniques that are used to solve problems in chemistry.
The most common computational technique that is used in chemistry is known as molecular dynamics. This method is used to simulate the behavior of molecules in order to understand how they will interact with each other. Another common computational technique is known as quantum mechanics/molecular mechanics (QM/MM). This method is used to simulate the behavior of molecules in order to understand how they will interact with each other on a quantum level.
There are many other computational techniques that can be used to solve problems in chemistry. These include methods such as density functional theory (DFT), ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD), and mean field theory (MFT).

Stan Mack (born Stanley David Mack; November 14, 1922 – August 11, 2018) was an American comic book writer and artist, best known for his work on the Marvel Comics characters The Thing and The Human Torch, as well as for co-creating (with artist Gene Colan) the character of Captain Marvel, Jr. Mack began his comics career in 1940, working for DC Comics on characters including the Flash and Green Lantern. He then moved to Marvel predecessor Atlas Comics, working on a variety of characters, including the Thing and the Torch, and co-creating Captain Marvel, Jr. He went on to draw The Human Torch stories in the 1960s and 1970s, and also worked on Captain Marvel and the Marvel Family from the 1970s to the 1980s. In the 1990s, Mack worked with artist George Tuska on the character of the Thing in issues of Marvel's The Avengers. Mack's artwork has been the subject of museum exhibitions, including the 2012 exhibition "The Art of the Captain Marvel Family" at the Legion of Super-Heroes Museum in Lincoln, Nebraska.

How to Make Your Excel Curve Solid or Transparent
 Excel has several different graph types that you can use to display your data. Each graph type has a different look and feel, and some may be more suitable than others for your needs. In addition, Excel allows you to customize the look of your graphs, so you can make them more or less transparent, add grid lines, and so on.
To make an Excel curve solid or transparent, you will need to access the Graphical Effects settings. These settings allow you to adjust the fill color and transparency of the graph.
Open the Excel file containing the graph you want to adjust.
Select the graph by clicking on it.
Click on the Graph tab at the top of the screen.
In the Graphical Effects group, click on the icon that looks like a paintbrush.
A menu will appear with several different options. Click on the Fill Effects option.
A new window will open with several different fill options. If you want to make the graph more transparent, select the Transparency option and then move the slider to the right. If you want to make the graph solid, move the slider to the left.
Once you have made your changes, click on the OK button to save them.

The electoral college system, which assigns votes based on the popularity of candidates in each state rather than a direct popular vote for president, is flawed and undermines the democratic process. Contrary to arguments defending the system, the electoral college does not truly represent the will of the people as it fails to consider the voting power of all citizens equally.
One prominent flaw of the electoral college is its disregard for voter equality. Unlike a direct popular vote system, where each vote carries equal weight, the electoral college gives less populous states disproportionately more influence. This is exemplified in scenarios where a vote in Wyoming, with its relatively small population, carries more weight in electing the president than a vote from a densely populated state like California. This inherent inequality undermines the principle of democracy, which posits that every citizen's opinion should be of equal value.
Another issue with the electoral college is its inherent bias towards rural states. This is largely due to the winner-takes-all approach wherein all of a state's electoral votes are awarded to the candidate who wins the state's popular vote. This method disadvantages voters in urban areas who may be more politically diverse and exacerbates the political divide between rural and urban America. In a direct popular vote system, however, the votes of urban dwellers would have a greater impact in densely populated areas, potentially leading to greater political representation for cities.
Defenders of the electoral college often cite the notion of guarding against "fringe" candidates and maintaining a strong presidency as benefits of the system. However, as evidenced by the 2016 election, the electoral college does not effectively prevent the election of candidates who do not have broad national support. Additionally, the system's emphasis on state-level victories can lead to a lack of attention to local issues, potentially weakening the overall democratic process.
In contrast, a direct popular vote system would mitigate the inequalities inherent in the electoral college system and better reflect the will of the people. By casting aside the state-based winner-takes-all approach, a direct popular vote would empower voters on both a local and national level, addressing the disparities that currently exist.
In conclusion, the electoral college system is not only flawed in its disregard for voter equality but also fails to effectively prevent the election of fringe candidates. Its bias towards rural states and potential weakening of attention to local issues further undermine its legitimacy. For these reasons, it is imperative that we adopt a direct popular vote system to ensure that the voice of every citizen is heard and to strengthen the integrity of our democratic process.

The electron doping of undoped high-$T_c$ cuprates via the transfer of chargefrom manganites (or other oxides) using heterostructure geometries is heretheoretically discussed. This possibility is mainly addressed via a detailedanalysis of photoemission and diffusion voltage experiments, which locate theFermi level of manganites above the bottom of the upper Hubbard band of somecuprate parent compounds. A diagram with the relative location of Fermi levelsand gaps for several oxides is presented. The procedure discussed here isgeneric, allowing for the qualitative prediction of the charge flow directionat several oxide interfaces. The addition of electrons to antiferromagnetic Cuoxides may lead to a superconducting state at the interface with minimalquenched disorder. Model calculations using static and dynamical mean-fieldtheory, supplemented by a Poisson equation formalism to address chargeredistribution at the interface, support this view. The magnetic state of themanganites could be antiferromagnetic or ferromagnetic. The former is better toinduce superconductivity than the latter, since the spin-polarized chargetransfer will be detrimental to singlet superconductivity. It is concluded thatin spite of the robust Hubbard gaps, the electron doping of undoped cuprates atinterfaces appears possible, and its realization may open an exciting area ofresearch in oxide heterostructures.

Well, it's one thing to be off by a day or so, but by a year? That's a massive mistake for an entire population to make. But it wasn't until 525 AD that the AD numbering system began. And there are a couple discrepancies in figuring out when Jesus was born, to start counting. The gospels (for those who take them literally) describe two things that are mentioned elsewhere and let us date it. Matthew says that after Jesus was born, King Herod (the Great) is still alive, because he massacred children in an attempt to kill Jesus. (There's no evidence of any such massacre, though Herod was very bloody). Luke says that Mary and Joseph were in Bethlehem only because of the Census of Quirinius. (That they would have to travel to Joseph's ancestral home of Bethlehem instead of staying in Nazareth makes no sense. The Romans would never have demanded that, and it's completely impractical.) ANYWAY... King Herod died in 4 BC, and the Census took place in 6/7 AD. So one, if not both of these is wrong. And if either of them is right, then our AD 1 is wrong. Basically, though the modern consensus is that Jesus was a real person, any attempt to figure out what year he was actually born in is a shot in the dark. So in that sense, we don't know if this really "should" be 2012 or not. But since 525, when they started the AD system, no. No, there's no doubt that it's been kept correctly. And there are enough documents and histories for the previous thousand years that when we label something "44 BC" we're confident that they didn't miss a year.
tl;dr- Year 1 AD is arbitrary, and frankly just a guess as to Jesus's birth year, but accepting that, yes, this is 2012.

The extraordinary astrometric accuracy of radio interferometry creates animportant and unique opportunity for the discovery and characterization ofexo-planets. Currently, the Very Long Baseline Array can routinely achievebetter than 100 microarcsecond accuracy, and can approach 10 microarcsecondwith careful calibration. We describe here RIPL, the Radio InterferometricPLanet search, a new program with the VLBA and the Green Bank 100 m telescopethat will survey 29 low-mass, active stars over 3 years with sub-Jovian planetmass sensitivity at 1 AU. An upgrade of the VLBA bandwidth will increaseastrometric accuracy by an order of magnitude. Ultimately, the colossalcollecting area of the Square Kilometer Array could push astrometric accuracyto 1 microarcsecond, making detection and characterizaiton of Earth massplanets possible. RIPL and other future radio astrometric planet searches occupy a uniquevolume in planet discovery and characterization parameter space. The parameterspace of astrometric searches gives greater sensitivity to planets at largeradii than radial velocity searches. For the VLBA and the expanded VLBA, thetargets of radio astrometric surveys are by necessity nearby, low-mass, activestars, which cannot be studied efficiently through the radial velocity method,coronagraphy, or optical interferometry. For the SKA, detection sensitivitywill extend to solar-type stars. Planets discovered through radio astrometricmethods will be suitable for characterization through extreme adaptive optics. The complementarity of radio astrometric techniques with other methodsdemonstrates that radio astrometry can play an important role in the roadmapfor exoplanet discovery and characterization.

Kaira, sometimes called frilled orbweavers, is a mostly neotropical genus of orb-weaver spiders first described by O. Pickard-Cambridge in 1889. It includes sixteen described species that occur from South America up to the southern and eastern USA. It is presumably related to Aculepeira, Amazonepeira and Metepeira. They spin small webs from which they hang upside down and attract male moths that fly into a basket formed by their legs. They use a moth pheromone for this, which resembles the one used by the bolas spiders of the genus Mastophora. Though they belong to the same family, the two genera are not closely related, so this is likely an example of convergent evolution. All species are pale yellow-white with scattered, small, white, brown and black random spots, or in some species transverse bands. Females have a body length of about. Males are less than half the size of females and less pigmented. Kaira specimens are uncommon in arachnologist collections, and the females of different species are difficult to separate. Females and immatures can be confused with species of the not closely related genus Pozonia. When a fly was put into a jar containing a K. alba, the female dropped from the underside of the lid on what seemed a single thread about long and hung there until the fly blundered into her. Then she clamped her legs around it and killed it. Instead of constructing orb webs, they construct a small trapezoidal web, containing two triangular zigzags of threads, which is remade every twenty minutes. The spider then hangs upside-down by the fourth leg on the lower and shorter parallel edge of the trapezoid, which is spread by the other legs. When a moth flies into the basket formed by the spider's legs, it is clasped and bitten, and later wrapped in araneid-like fashion. The moth is then hung from a trapeze line between the last legs of the spider, which resumes the hunting posture. As many as eight moths can be caught in this way before the spider starts feeding. Kaira altiventer O. Pickard-Cambridge, 1889 – USA to Brazil Kaira candidissima (Mello-Leitão, 1941) – Argentina Kaira conica Gerschman & Schiapelli, 1948 – Brazil, Argentina Kaira gibberosa O. Pickard-Cambridge, 1890 – Mexico to Brazil Kaira sexta (Chamberlin, 1916) – Guatemala to Brazil

Thousands of years ahead? Try billions of years.
A solar system capable of supporting life needs to be formed from the material from a dead star. Most stars last for several billion years, but really massive stars can have lifespans of only a few million years. The universe had 9 billion years to develop before our sun came along. Our sun has been here for a bit over 4.5 billion years (just a little older than the Earth). Our sun is believed to have formed partly from the remnants of a nearby supernova. This may have been due to the explosion of a short-lived massive star. The oldest star we know of was born when the universe was around half a billion years old, and the Milky Way galaxy has been around for about the same amount of time. It is reasonable to assume that it is possible for a new solar system to form out of the residue within a similar time frame. That leaves us with 8 billion years for a really massive star to form, go through its entire life, and end with a supernova. That's entirely possible, because as I said, really massive stars can have lifespans as short as a few million years. The bigger the star, the shorter it's life.
It took our solar system 4.5 billion years to form intelligent life, but even there, there's some leeway. It took Earth 800 million years, but it's conceivable that some other planet, with slightly different conditions could develop life a hundred million years or so quicker. Once the first cells formed, it took about 2 and a half billion to 3 billion years before multicellular organisms appeared. I don't know much about the biology, or the probabilities of it happening, but is it not possible that this could have happened on another planet maybe half a billion, or even a billion years quicker? Mammals lived under the dinosaurs for almost 150 million years. Imagine if the dinosaurs went extinct 100 million years sooner? A 3 billion year old planet could possibly reach our level.
There's so much leeway here. There's no reason a species of our technology level could not have existed 5 billion years ago, more than 5 billion light years away, and we just can't see it yet.
